pages.full_text:
"More class than well in __Hardwick Hall__. We looked at the gardens + the mixed old Hall. Tints kept pulling on the [?lead]. Very cold wind. Ferrari's + Poshes on the carpet. Charles [?Batto]. Peter called. Boss of Hardwick's granddaughter Arbella kept a prisoner because a threat to [?Eliza]. Bloody furnishings. Well laid out + cared for.

Off 2:30 pm after a good lunch We looked round house + now going home.
pages.notes: Text is written at approximately 90 degrees rotation (the page appears to be used in landscape orientation). A large printed illustrated map/aerial-view diagram of what appears to be Hardwick Hall and its grounds is glued onto the page. The name 'Arbella' likely refers to Arbella Stuart, who was kept at Hardwick Hall. '[?Eliza]' could be 'Eliza' or 'Eliz' (short for Elizabeth). 'Charles [?Batto]' — second word uncertain, could be 'Batto', 'Lotto', or another surname. 'Ferrari's + Poshes' likely refers to expensive cars seen in the car park. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
Friday April 24th
   We left at 5.30 + reached The White House at Ross at 6.45. It's on the side of the hill overlooking the Wye. In the 1700s a benefactor called Kyrle did alot of good works beautifying the town. The B+B owner spent ages mentioning every restaurant in Ross + whether they [?are good] [illegible]

We walked from this view-point Yat Rock down to river + up again.

hrs.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '68' visible in top-right corner. A postcard of 'THE RIVER WYE SYMONDS YAT' is glued into the lower right portion of the page, with handwritten text continuing in the narrow left margin beside it. 'April 24th Friday' fell on a Friday in 2009 and 2015 (within volume B's 2003-2019 range); year cannot be determined from this page alone. The B+B 'The White House at Ross' is in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire. 'Kyrle' refers to John Kyrle (1637-1724), the 'Man of Ross'. Some text is partially obscured by the postcard and difficult to read in the gutter area — 'they [?are good]' is uncertain. 'hrs.' at bottom likely an abbreviation left incomplete.
